HBTU Students Secure 4 Placements at GMM Non-Stick Coatings

Training & Placement Cell HBTU Kanpur (HBTI Kanpur) is delighted to announce that 4 outstanding students from the Batch of 2026 have successfully secured offers at GMM Non-Stick Coatings, a specialized leader in advanced coating solutions and surface engineering.

🌟 Heartiest congratulations to our proud achievers:

Anand Prakash (B.Tech. CHE)
Sikander Ahmad (B.Tech. CHE)
Rupal Yadav (M.Tech. CHE)
Gauri Shukla (M.Tech. CHE)

🏆 As students from the 2022–2026 batch at Harcourt Butler Technical University, Kanpur, securing an offer at GMM Non-Stick Coatings — a name synonymous with precision surface engineering, technical innovation, and manufacturing excellence — is a testament to your technical brilliance, forward-thinking mindset, and relentless pursuit of excellence. This achievement marks the beginning of what promises to be an extraordinary professional journey. You have made the entire HBTU family immensely proud! 🎖️

The HBTU fraternity extends its warmest congratulations and wishes each of these achievers a future filled with growth, purpose, and success.

We also express our heartfelt gratitude to GMM Non-Stick Coatings for recognizing the caliber of HBTU graduates and for fostering a strong and meaningful industry–academia partnership.

✅ Why Choose HBTU?
✅ Among the top Engineering colleges in India
✅ All students are top rankers of JEE Mains
✅ Ranked 1st in IIRF State Rankings (2025)
✅ Ranked 19th in India Today Survey (2024)
✅ NAAC Accredited with A+ Grade
✅ 10 Departments NBA Accredited

🎓 HBTU Kanpur Celebrates a Historic Placement Season | Batch of 2026

The Training & Placement Cell of Harcourt Butler Technical University (HBTU), Kanpur, is proud to celebrate the remarkable milestones achieved during the Campus Placement Season for the graduating Batch of 2026.

Established in 1921, HBTU continues its century-long legacy of academic and technical excellence. Our NAAC A+ accredited institution has once again demonstrated its strong academic foundation and industry readiness, resulting in a landmark recruitment season.

📊 Historic Placement Highlights

🌟 700+ Total Placement Offers
🏢 180+ Recruiting Organizations

📈 Placement Tier-Wise Highlights

Our esteemed recruiters have extended highly competitive offers across multiple salary tiers, reflecting strong industry demand for HBTU talent:

🚀 15+ LPA Tier: 40+ Students Placed | 10+ Recruiting Companies
💎 12+ LPA Tier: 60+ Students Placed | 15+ Recruiting Companies
📈 10+ LPA Tier: 70+ Students Placed | 20+ Recruiting Companies
💼 8+ LPA Tier: 220+ Students Placed | 50+ Recruiting Companies

🤝 Our Heartfelt Gratitude to Our Alumni Network & Recruiters

The Training & Placement Cell extends its sincere gratitude to our esteemed recruiters, corporate partners, and our global HBTU Alumni Network for their continued trust, collaboration, and support. Your confidence in HBTU students and your commitment to nurturing young talent through meaningful career opportunities have been invaluable. We truly appreciate your partnership and look forward to strengthening these relationships in the years ahead.

🎯 Under Visionary Leadership & Guidance

This historic milestone was achieved under the leadership of Prof. Samsher, Hon'ble Vice Chancellor; Prof. Vivek Kumar, Dean, Planning & Resource Generation; and under the strategic guidance of Prof. Naveen Kumar Gupta, Professor & Head, Training & Placement Cell. Their vision, dedication, and commitment to strengthening industry-academia collaboration have been instrumental in creating exceptional opportunities for our students.

🤝 Special Thanks to Our TPRs

Heartfelt appreciation to all our Student Training & Placement Coordinators (TPRs) for their unwavering dedication, professionalism, and tireless efforts throughout the season. Their seamless coordination and continuous support for recruiters, partners, and students ensured a successful recruitment cycle.

🚀 Looking Ahead

The T&P Cell remains committed to strengthening industry partnerships, expanding alumni engagement, and creating even greater opportunities for future HBTU graduates. We look forward to welcoming more organizations and continuing our tradition of excellence.

📧 placement@hbtu.ac.in
🌐 www.hbtu.ac.in

Warm regards
Prof. Naveen Kumar Gupta,
Head, Training & Placement Cell, HBTU Kanpur.

The Computer Science & Engineering Department at Harcourt Butler Technical University, Kanpur, in collaboration with The Institution of Engineers (India), hosted a masterclass in technological foresight: the national seminar on ‘Human-Centric and Responsible AI’ (HCRAI-2026).
It was a brilliant convergence of academia and industry, focused entirely on ensuring that our technological tomorrow remains anchored in human values.
A few profound insights that are still resonating with me:
🔹 Innovation Needs an Ethical Bedrock: Keynote speaker Yatindra Nath Singh (IIT Kanpur) set a powerful tone, reminding us that transparency and accountability aren't just compliance metrics they are the very foundation of sustainable AI.
🔹 The Next Frontier is Neural: The sessions on Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCI) were nothing short of visionary. Dr. Manoj Kumar Gupta (SMVDU) and Dr. Avinash Singh (UTS, Sydney) painted a fascinating picture of how human-machine coordination will soon redefine the landscapes of healthcare, education, and intelligent systems.
🔹 Bridging Theory and Enterprise: Industry veteran Mr. Deepak Agarwal delivered a crucial reality check from the corporate frontline. His message was clear: in the era of digital transformation, responsible AI isn't just an ethical choice—it is the ultimate driver of effective technical leadership and enterprise success.
Orchestrating a dialogue of this caliber requires immense vision. A massive congratulations to the leadership at HBTU for facilitating this platform, including Hon'ble Vice-Chancellor Prof. Samsher Gautam, Prof. Anita Yadav (HoD, CSE), Dr. Raghuraj Singh (Chairman, IEI Kanpur & HCRAI-2026), and Prof. Prabhat Verma (Convener).

A special note of gratitude goes out to the Danish Ather Ph.D. Postdoc, Malaysia and our esteemed CSE faculty members Prof. Vandana Dixit Kaushik, Dr. Bharat Bhushan Sagar, Dr. Imran Khan, and Prof. Rashi Agarwal—whose active presence, guidance, and unwavering support were instrumental in making this event a resounding success. 👏

With over 30 groundbreaking research papers presented, one thing is certain: technology dictates what is possible, but humanity must dictate what is right.
Excited to see how these conversations shape the future of our industry! 🌍🤝
hashtag#ResponsibleAI hashtag#HumanCentricTech hashtag#HBTUKanpur hashtag#ArtificialIntelligence hashtag#TechLeadership hashtag#AIEthics

𝗙𝗖𝗜𝗧 𝗦𝗵𝗼𝘄𝗰𝗮𝘀𝗲𝘀 𝗜𝗻𝗻𝗼𝘃𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗮𝘁 𝗔𝗔𝗥𝗔𝗠𝗕𝗛'𝟮𝟲 🎯

The Fraternity Of Computer Science and Information Technology (FCIT) is proud to share the success of our participation in AARAMBH’26, the flagship Innovation & Entrepreneurship Summit at HBTU Kanpur.

Under the banner of 𝗩𝗜𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗔𝗥 𝗦𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘁𝘂𝗽 𝗦𝗵𝗼𝘄𝗰𝗮𝘀𝗲 & 𝗜𝗻𝗻𝗼𝘃𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗚𝗮𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗿𝘆, our students demonstrated that the Department of Computer Science & Engineering is a hub for both cutting edge software and advanced hardware integration.
It was a privilege to host and demonstrate our projects to the 𝗛𝗼𝗻’𝗯𝗹𝗲 𝗩𝗶𝗰𝗲 𝗖𝗵𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲𝗹𝗹𝗼𝗿 𝗼𝗳 𝗛𝗕𝗧𝗨 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗖𝗼𝗺𝗺𝗶𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗲𝗿 𝗼𝗳 𝗞𝗮𝗻𝗽𝘂𝗿, who lauded the technical proficiency and entrepreneurial spirit of our students.


🛠️ 𝗛𝗶𝗴𝗵𝗹𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁𝘀 𝗳𝗿𝗼𝗺 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗙𝗖𝗜𝗧 𝗦𝘁𝗮𝗹𝗹:

• 𝗔𝗜 𝗛𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗥𝗼𝘃𝗲𝗿: OpenCV-based gesture control systems.
• 𝗧-𝗥𝗘𝗫: Our Bot Soccer Champion (Resonance winner).
• 𝗖𝘂𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗺 𝗗𝗿𝗼𝗻𝗲 & 𝗥𝗼𝗯𝗼𝘁𝗶𝗰 𝗔𝗿𝗺𝘀: Showcasing precision engineering.
• 𝗣𝗿𝗼𝗽𝗜𝘁: A competitive strategy game developed by our first-year talent.

This success was made possible under the visionary guidance of our HoD, Dr. Anita Yadav Ma'am, and the dedicated efforts of our core coordination team, including Mubashir Sofi,Sahib Singh, Aman Singh & Abhilash Jaiswal.

𝗦𝗽𝗲𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝗠𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝘁𝗼 𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗠𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗼𝗿𝘀:
The success of the FCIT stall was guided by the vision of our faculty. We extend our deepest gratitude to:

• 𝗗𝗿.𝗔𝗻𝗶𝘁𝗮 𝗬𝗮𝗱𝗮𝘃 𝗠𝗮'𝗮𝗺 (𝗛𝗼𝗗,𝗖𝗦𝗘)
For her constant support and being the pillar of the department.

• 𝗠𝗿.𝗦𝗶𝗱𝗱𝗵𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗵 𝗦𝗶𝗿
Our Stall Incharge, whose meticulous coordination ensured a seamless exhibition.

• 𝗣𝗿𝗼𝗳.𝗕.𝗕.𝗦𝗮𝗴𝗮𝗿 𝗦𝗶𝗿
For his invaluable guidance and motivation throughout the event.

• 𝗗𝗿. Rashi Agarwal 𝗠𝗮’𝗮𝗺 𝗔𝗻𝗱 𝗗𝗿. 𝗩𝗮𝗻𝗱𝗮𝗻𝗮 𝗗𝗶𝘅𝗶𝘁 𝗠𝗮’𝗮𝗺
For their constant encouragement and insights.

At FCIT, we don't just code the future we build it.

🚀 𝗔𝗟𝗚𝗢𝗥𝗛𝗬𝗧𝗛𝗠 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟲 - 𝗙𝗖𝗜𝗧 𝗢𝗿𝗶𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, 𝗛𝗕𝗧𝗨 𝗞𝗮𝗻𝗽𝘂𝗿

Organized by the Fraternity of Computer Science and Information Technology (FCIT), HBTU Kanpur, the event kicked off a new chapter of innovation, creativity, and collaboration,𝘵𝘩𝘪𝘴 𝘸𝘢𝘴𝘯’𝘵 𝘫𝘶𝘴𝘵 𝘢𝘯 𝘰𝘳𝘪𝘦𝘯𝘵𝘢𝘵𝘪𝘰𝘯. 𝘐𝘵 𝘸𝘢𝘴 𝘵𝘩𝘦 𝘳𝘦𝘷𝘪𝘷𝘢𝘭 𝘰𝘧 𝘢 𝘭𝘦𝘨𝘢𝘤𝘺.

Each activity aimed to introduce students to the fraternity’s vision, inspire creativity, showcase technical skills, and foster a sense of community and collaboration from the very start.

𝗞𝗲𝘆 𝗧𝗮𝗸𝗲𝗮𝘄𝗮𝘆𝘀:

● 𝗜𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗮𝗰𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝗜𝗰𝗲-𝗕𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗸𝗲𝗿 𝗤𝘂𝗶𝘇 : Sparked connections, confidence, and energy right from the start.
● 𝗛𝗮𝗻𝗱𝘀-𝗢𝗻 𝗔𝗜 𝗖𝗼𝗺𝗽𝗲𝘁𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻: Image generation, PDF creation, and presentations brought technology to life; winners were awarded certificates and cash prizes.
● 𝗜𝗻𝗻𝗼𝘃𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗼𝗻 𝗗𝗶𝘀𝗽𝗹𝗮𝘆: projects by students demonstrated creativity and problem-solving skills.
● 𝗖𝘂𝗹𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗹 𝗘𝗱𝗴𝗲 : Music and dance added vibrance to the event.
● 𝗖𝗼𝗺𝗺𝘂𝗻𝗶𝘁𝘆 & 𝗚𝗿𝗼𝘄𝘁𝗵 : Collaboration, teamwork, and belonging in FCIT are just as important as coding skills.

A sincere thank you to Dr. Anita Yadav ma'am (HoD, CSE), Dr. Siddharth Srivastava (Supervisor) , all faculty members, HRMAC Technologies Pvt. Ltd. (Sponsor) , and every student who worked behind the scenes to make this event seamless and impactful.

🔥 𝗜𝗻 𝗮 𝗻𝘂𝘁𝘀𝗵𝗲𝗹𝗹, 𝗶𝘁 𝘄𝗮𝘀 𝗮 𝗺𝗼𝘃𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁, 𝗮 𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗺𝘂𝗻𝗶𝘁𝘆, 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗮 𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗮𝗰𝘆 𝗿𝗲𝗯𝗼𝗿𝗻.

Phase 2 of the Campus Placement Season (January–June) for the Batch of 2026 has officially begun at HBTU Kanpur.

The commencement of Phase 2 follows a strong and steady Phase 1 (August–December), marked by active industry participation and diverse placement opportunities.

Phase 1 at a glance:
🔹 The highest compensation offered reached up to 20 LPA
🔹 Multiple roles were offered in the 15–20 LPA compensation range
🔹 The average CTC stood at 9.2 LPA
🔹 200+ students secured placement offers
🔹 75+ organizations participated in the placement process
🔹 Opportunities spanned software, data & AI/ML, analytics, cloud, core engineering and chemical engineering domains

With Phase 2 now underway, we look forward to continued collaboration with recruiters and creating further opportunities for our students in the months ahead.

For recruitment and collaboration queries, please connect with us at placement@hbtu.ac.in.

Student Coordinators:
Om Singh Baghel - +91 86043 36756
Vishwajeet Yadav - +91 88814 77363

Warm regards,
Dr. Naveen Kumar Gupta
Professor, Training & Placement
Harcourt Butler Technical University, Kanpur

The Placement Summary for the Batch of 2025 at HBTU Kanpur reflects a strong and steady placement season, marked by active industry participation and diverse opportunities across core engineering and technology domains.

With 24 reputed organizations participating in the recruitment process, students secured roles spanning core chemical engineering, consulting, and allied sectors, reinforcing the institute’s position as a reliable talent hub.

In a few months, this placement season will be wrapping up. As we approach its conclusion, we are glad to present this report-cum-brochure to reach out to our esteemed recruiters, showcasing key data and insights. While this may come a little later than usual, our aim is to provide a comprehensive overview of the placement scenario for the Chemical Engineering branch.

Batch of 2025 – Highlights at a Glance:
🔹 Total Students Placed: 71
🔹 Total Participating Companies: 24
🔹 Highest Package Offered: ₹13.5 LPA
🔹 Median Package Offered: ₹7.0 LPA
🔹 Highest Recruiter Contribution: Reliance Industries Limited
🔹 Other Top Recruiters: Jubilant Ingrevia Limited, ExxonMobil, Lummus Technology, SAMSUNG E&A INDIA, PI Industries Ltd.
The placement drive also witnessed consistent hiring across organizations such as Aarti Industries Ltd., Indorama Corporation, Krishak Bharati Cooperative Limited (KRIBHCO), Tata Advanced Systems Limited, SRF Limited, and others, highlighting a well-distributed and competitive recruitment landscape.

The structured placement procedure ensured transparency, efficiency, and seamless coordination between students and recruiters, enabling meaningful outcomes for all stakeholders.

Warm regards,
Prof. GL Devnani(Head, Chemical Engineering Department)
Dr. Ashwini Sood(Professor, Chemical Engineering Department)
Prof Naveen Kumar Gupta(Training and Placement Officer)
Dr. Pranava Chaudhari(Assistant Professor, Chemical Engineering Department)
Harcourt Butler Technical University
